utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi keith , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ah , not too good . my blood sugar is n't under control .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , uh , so keith is a 58-year-old male here for evaluation of high blood sugar . so , what happened ? ha- have you just been taking your blood sugars at home and noticed that they're really high ? or ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i've been taking them at home and i feel like they've been creeping up slightly .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have- ... what have they been running , in like the 200's or 300's ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: they've been running in the 300's ? and tell me about your diet . have you been eating anything to spark- ... spike them up ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: to be honest my diet has n't changed much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . have you- ... go ahead .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: actually it has n't changed at all . much of the same .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and what do you con- consider the same ? are you eating lots of sugar ? like , teas and coffees and-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't take sugar with my tea .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and how about , um , like any added sugars into any kind of processed foods or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i think most of my sugars come from fruit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: from what ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: fruit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: fruit , okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , and have you been feeling sick recently ? have you had any fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i have not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: body aches , joint pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , a bit of joint pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: multiple joints , or just one joint ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , my knee . uh , sorry , right knee to be more exact .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your right knee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and what happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ah , to be honest , nothing much . i just noticed it when you said it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . um , and how about any nausea or vomiting or belly pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i was nauseous a couple of days back but , uh , that's just because i was sitting in the back of a car . i hate that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and no burning when you urinate or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , um ... you know , i know that you've had this reflux in the past . how are you doing with that ? are you still having a lot of reflux symptoms or do you feel like it's better since we've put you on the protonix ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it's a bit better . uh , i do n't get up at night anymore with reflux and that's always a good thing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and i know you have this history of congestive heart failure . have you noticed any recent , uh , weight gain or fluid retention ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , not really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no ? okay . um , and any problems sleeping while laying flat ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i- i prefer to sleep on my side so i ca n't really say .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , but even then , you're flat .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and i know that we had an issue with your right rotator cuff , is that okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's surprisingly good now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . all right , well let's go ahead and we'll do a quick physical exam . so ... feeling your neck , i do feel like your thyroid's a bit enlarged here . um , your heart is nice and regular . your lungs are clear . your abdomen , um , is nice and soft . your right knee shows that you have some erythema and- and an insect bite with associated fluctuants . and , um , you have some lower extremity edema on the right hand side . so let's go ahead and look at some of your results . i know the nurse had reported these things and we ordered some labs on you before you came in . hey dragon , show me the vital signs . okay , well your- your vital signs look good , which is good . hey dragon , show me the lyme titer . okay , so , you know , your lyme titer is a little elevated , so i think we'll have to go ahead and- and look into that a little bit , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: makes sense .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that can certainly cause your blood sugar to be elevated . um , hey dragon , uh , show me the rapid strep . and you also have , uh , positive for strep . so i think we have some reasons as to why your blood sugar is so high . so my impression of you , you know , you have this hyperglycemia , which is probably related to some infections going on in your body . um , from a- a- a rapid strep standpoint we're gon na go ahead and treat you with penicillin or , i'm sorry , amoxicillin , 500 milligrams , three times a day . uh , make sure you take it all , even if you start feeling better , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: for sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order amoxicillin , 500 milligrams , three times a day for 10 days . um ... okay . and from ... , and from all- ... a positive lyme titer aspect , we should go ahead and order a western blot , just pcr to see if you have any , um , to see if it's actually acute lyme , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , hey dragon , order a western blot pcr for lyme . okay . all right . well we'll go ahead and , um , the nurse will come in soon and she'll set you up with these tests , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . you said lyme . is that related to lyme disease ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes it is , yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you're certain i do n't have alpha-gal syndrome though , right ? i'm terrified of that one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have what ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alpha-gal syndrome , the one where a tick bites you and you get an allergic reaction to meat .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i do n't think so . have you eaten meat over the last couple of days ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well i- ... it's , you know , your blood sugar's elevated so you might be having an inflammatory response to that , but we'll go ahead and order some tests to look into it , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . call me if anything happens , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: definitely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: Hyperglycemia
The patient is a 58-year-old male presenting for evaluation of high blood sugar.
The doctor asks if the patient has been monitoring blood sugar levels at home.
The patient reports blood sugar levels have been increasing.
The doctor asks for the specific range of the patient's home blood sugar readings.
The patient reports blood sugar readings in the 300s.
The doctor asks about the patient's diet and potential triggers for high blood sugar.
The patient reports no significant changes to his diet.
The doctor asks if the patient consumes high amounts of sugar in tea or coffee.
The patient denies adding sugar to his tea.
The doctor asks about the consumption of processed foods with added sugars.
The patient believes his sugar intake is primarily from fruit.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced fever or chills.
The patient denies fever or chills.
The doctor asks if the patient has body aches or joint pain.
The patient reports experiencing joint pain.
The doctor asks if the joint pain is localized to one or multiple joints.
The patient reports pain in his right knee.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain.
The patient reports recent nausea attributed to motion sickness while riding in a car.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced dysuria.
The patient denies burning with urination.
Reflux
The doctor asks about the status of the patient's reflux symptoms.
The patient reports improvement in reflux and no longer wakes up at night due to symptoms.
Congestive Heart Failure
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced recent weight gain or fluid retention.
The patient denies recent weight gain or fluid retention.
The doctor asks if the patient has difficulty sleeping while lying flat.
The patient prefers sleeping on his side.
Rotator cuff
The doctor asks about the status of the patient's right rotator cuff issue.
The patient reports the rotator cuff is doing well.

Physical exam findings include mild thyromegaly, clear lungs, regular heart rhythm, soft abdomen, right knee erythema with insect bite and fluctuance, and trace right lower extremity edema; Lyme titer is elevated.
The patient tested positive for strep; the doctor attributes hyperglycemia to an inflammatory response from infections.
Amoxicillin 500mg three times a day.
The doctor orders a western blot PCR for Lyme disease.
The patient asks if the elevated titer is related to Lyme disease.
The patient asks if he has alpha-gal syndrome.
The doctor asks if the patient has consumed meat recently.
The patient confirms he has consumed meat.

case_description: high blood sugar; reflux; hx CHF; right rotator cuff issue
